ChangeRequest.name: Considerations regarding the `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` entity type
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview
The added value of the introduction of the entity type `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` is unclear.
Furthermore, it seems that the introduction of this entity type implies a duplication, namely the `Reverse repurchase agreement cash leg` and `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` as well as `Repurchase agreement cash leg` and `Deposit`.
### Contact person
dominik.lin-ully@oenb.at, sara.crestini@bdi.it
### Background information
The current model design of the `Instrument` entity type hierarchy reflects the different types of `Instrument`s, e.g., `Credit card debt`, as defined in the BIRD LDM (~input). The model construct of `Instrument role` allows to assign different roles to an `Instrument`, for example a `Credit card debt` that is an asset for the reporting agent would act in the `Financial asset instrument` role (or more accurately one of its subtypes). If we would have defined more specific `Instrument role`s instead of generic ones (see https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/issues/25), we would for example have something like `Financial asset credit card debt` and `Financial liability credit card debt` for `Credit card debt` or `Financial asset reverse repurchase agreement` and `Financial liability repurchase agreement` for `Repurchase agreement instrument`.
Based on the discussion related to AE requirements (see [AE Requirements - Deposits and Repurchase agreements](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/issues/462)), the new entity type `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` has been introduced (as a subtype of the `Instrument` hierarchy) including the relationship to the entity type `Reverse repurchase agreement cash leg`. Similarly the relationship between `Repurchase agreement cash leg` and `Deposit` has been established.
It seems to us that the only value added of integrating these data elements (i.e., entity type `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` and the relationships) is limited to the identification of the `Debtor` (for `Reverse repurchase agreement loan`s) and `Depositor` (for `Deposit`s).
If this information was required it may be covered by a different modeling construct, e.g., subtyping `Instrument role` for different `Instrument` types and adding a relationship between `Financial asset reverse repurchase agreement` and `Debtor` (of course not saying this is the best solution without knowing all the underlying requirements but just adding an example).
However, we do not see that this was the main purpose for the integration of these data elements (based on the discussion within the associated Gitlab issue). Furthermore, it seems that the integration of these data elements is in contrast to the applied modeling construct (using the `Instrument role` entity type hierarchy) for representing assets and liabilities and / or duplicates information already present in the model.
Our understanding is that a `Reverse repurchase agreement instrument`, if it is considered an asset, would act in the `Financial asset instrument` role (or one of its subtypes) - similarly to a `Credit card debt` considered an asset. Hence, according to our understanding, a `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` is already represented in the BIRD LDM by an `Instrument` of type `Repurchase agreement instrument` which acts in the `Financial asset instrument` role (or one of its subtypes). If our understanding is accurate, this would also result in additional referential integrity constraints / business rules for the current model design. For example the fact that the `Debtor` (and also `Creditor`) of a `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` must be either the `Buyer` or `Seller` of a `Repurchase agreement instrument`; similar considerations apply to the `Depositor` and the `Deposit taking corporation` of a `Deposit` (which is a `Repurchase agreement cash leg`). Also consider that a `Repurchase agreement instrument` must not be a `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` and a `Deposit` (being a `Repurchase agreement cash leg`) at the same time, etc.
Another 'reason' why we believe this design is flawed is that the 'container' and the 'items' are of the same type - i.e., the fact that `Reverse repurchase agreement loan`, `Deposit` (being a `Repurchase agreement cash leg`) and `Repurchase agreement instrument` are subtypes of `Instrument`, which implies that they are each identifiable by their `Instrument identifier` and are therefore (from a data modeling perspective) of the same type. For us this is quite odd, because (normally) a 'group of persons' is different to a 'person' - but of course we are happy to be convinced otherwise.
Furthermore, what `Instrument role` does the `Repurchase agreement instrument` act in? Because of the introduction of `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` as an individual `Instrument` type and the `Deposit` (being the `Repurchase agreement cash leg`) as an individual `Instrument` type it wouldn't make sense that the `Repurchase agreement instrument` acts as a `Financial asset instrument` or a `Financial liability instrument` (these roles should most likely be assigned to the `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` and `Deposit`. Also the other `Instrument role` types do not match - at least in my opinion. However the `Instrument role` is mandatory for each `Instrument`...
### Points/Questions to resolve
* what problem is / was solved by the introduction of the entity type `Repurchase agreement instrument`? * does the introduction of this entity type and the associated relationships (between `Reverse repurchase agreement cash leg` and `Reverse repurchase agreement loan` as well as `Repurchase agreement cash leg` and `Deposit`) imply a duplication? * is the introduction of this entity type consistent with the application of the 'role construct' (i.e., `Instrument` acts in `Instrument role`) as regards the representation of `Instrument`(s) being `Financial asset instrument`(s) and / or `Financial liability instrument`(s)?
we kindly ask the WS DM to reflect on these considerations, also considering the above stated questions and - if considered beneficial - to adjust the BIRD (E)LDM accordingly.
### Related issues
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
